DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CLEMENT JONES' MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS UNOPPOSED. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 27, 2020 TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER ONE FURTHER OPPORTUNITY TO RESPOND TO THE DISCOVERY REFERENCED IN THIS COURT'S ORDER DATED JUNE 30, 2020. IF VERIFIED RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION ARE NOT SERVED PRIOR TO THE CONTINUED DATE OF HEARING THE MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS WILL BE GRANTED. THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO APPEAR ON THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE TO ADVISE THE STATUS OF THE DISCOVERY RESPONSES. A COPY OF THIS ORDER SHALL BE SERVED ON THE PLAINTIFF WITH NOTICE OF ENTRY. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING VIA EMAIL TO SBS@SBSLAWSF.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVE STEIN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

DEFENDANTS MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO HIS SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, IS UNOPPOSED AND GRANTED. THE PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO SAID DISCOVERY REQUESTS WITHIN TWENTY DAYS OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER WITH NOTICE OF ENTRY. THE SPECIFIC DISCOVERY PLEADINGS REFERENCED IN THIS ORDER SHALL BE RE-SERVED ON THE PLAINTIFF ALONG WITH THIS ORDER. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN THE SUM OF $860.00 TO BE PAID BY PLAINTIFF WITHIN THE AFORESAID 20 DAYS. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-19-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CLEMENT JONES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is Off calendar as moot. The Court dismissed this action as a discovery termination sanction on October 27, 2020.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SPENCER PAVELCHIK, Esq. of Low McKinley & Salenko, LLP (Tel: 916-231-2400) for Defendant Clement K. Jones, M.D.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CLEMENT JONES' Motion For Terminating Sanctions.

Defense counsel stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and defense counsel each authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court discusses with defense counsel the required form of judicial findings and related dismissal order. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The motion for terminating sanctions is unopposed and the Court finds grounds for terminating the action based on violation of previous discovery orders.

Defense counsel is to prepare and email a proposed order to the plaintiff to invite comment and to cc the Judge Pro Tem on the email at sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-15-2020 Continued To Oct-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CLEMENT JONES' Motion For Terminating Sanctions:

Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is unopposed. The motion is continued to October 27, 2020 to allow plaintiff in pro per one further opportunity to respond to the discovery referenced in this court's order dated June 30, 2020. If verified responses without objection are not served prior to the continued date of hearing the motion for terminating sanctions will be granted. The parties are directed to appear on the continued hearing date to advise the status of the discovery responses. A copy of this order shall be served on the plaintiff with Notice of Entry.

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling via email to sbs@sbslawsf.com.
